phrases.cultural_note: Czech station staff often give concise directions using the underpass (podchod) as the main navigation point.

likely_response.local: Dolů do podchodu a potom doleva.
likely_response.translation_en: Down into the underpass and then left.

responses.english: Down into the underpass and then left.
responses.local: Dolů do podchodu a potom doleva.

phrases.intent_en: When does the next train to Prague leave
phrases.text_local: Kdy jede příští vlak do Prahy?
phrases.cultural_note: Times are given in 24-hour format. Staff often add the platform number for express trains.

likely_response.local: V deset hodin deset, nástupiště jedna.

responses.local: V deset hodin deset, nástupiště jedna.

phrases.intent_en: Is the underpass open all night
phrases.cultural_note: Main station underpasses are typically accessible 24/7 to allow platform access, though shops inside them close.

likely_response.translation_en: Yes, the underpass does not close.

responses.english: Yes, the underpass does not close.

phrases.intent_en: How to use the luggage storage
phrases.cultural_note: At many Czech stations, the luggage storage (úschovna zavazadel) is a staffed service rather than just automated lockers.

likely_response.local: Zaplaťte na pokladně, dostanete klíč od skříňky.
likely_response.translation_en: Pay at the counter, you will get a key for a locker.

responses.english: Pay at the counter, you will get a key for a locker.
responses.local: Zaplaťte na pokladně, dostanete klíč od skříňky.

phrases.intent_en: Where is the first class waiting room
phrases.cultural_note: Czech main stations typically have a dedicated 1st class waiting room (Čekárna 1. třídy) near the main ticket hall.

likely_response.translation_en: In the main hall, next to the ticket counters.

responses.english: In the main hall, next to the ticket counters.
